verse_specific_anchors: nistrai-guṇya — be beyond the three guṇas (the operative imperative)
nitya-sattva-stha — ever-poised in sattva (the contested term)
niryoga-kṣema — free from acquisition-and-preservation anxiety (specific compound)
verdict_reason: The engineering analog hangs on the three-guṇa-state framing (sattva-clarity / rajas-restlessness / tamas-inertia) and the niryoga-kṣema imperative (no anxiety about acquiring next quarter's wins or preserving last quarter's). That cluster is uniquely 2.45's. Could it come from chapter 14 (which elaborates the three guṇas systematically)? No — 14.5 onward describes the guṇas; only 2.45 issues the operative imperative to rise above them in the context of buddhi-yoga. Could it come from 2.48 (samatvam = yoga)? No — samatvam is evenness across pleasure/pain, not transcendence of guṇa-domains. The three-Mondays scenario specifically tracks the guṇa-classification and the niryoga-kṣema disposition. PASS for both 7.6 and 3.5.
